mapper.select: SELECT id, request_info_id, receipt_info_id, result_info_id, completion_info_id, user_id, product_id, stage, created_at, updated_at FROM tests WHERE id = #{id}
SELECT id, request_info_id, receipt_info_id, result_info_id, completion_info_id, user_id, product_id, stage, created_at, updated_at FROM tests WHERE stage = #{name}
mapper.update: UPDATE tests SET request_info_id = #{requestInfoId}, receipt_info_id = #{receiptInfoId}, result_info_id = #{resultInfoId}, completion_info_id = #{completionInfoId}, user_id = #{userId}, product_id = #{productId}, stage = #{stage}, updated_at = #{updatedAt} WHERE id = #{id}
mapper.select: SELECT id, request_info_id, receipt_info_id, result_info_id, completion_info_id, user_id, product_id, stage, created_at, updated_at FROM tests ORDER BY created_at DESC
SELECT id, request_info_id, receipt_info_id, result_info_id, completion_info_id, user_id, product_id, stage, created_at, updated_at FROM tests WHERE user_id = #{userId} ORDER BY created_at DESC
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tests WHERE stage IN ('REQUEST', 'RECEIPT', 'RECEIPT_APPROVAL', 'RESULT_INPUT')
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tests WHERE stage = 'COMPLETED'
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tests WHERE user_id = #{requesterId}
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tests WHERE stage = #{stage}

SELECT t.id, t.stage, t.created_at as createdAt, t.updated_at as updatedAt, ri.title, u.username as requesterName, p.name as productName FROM tests t LEFT JOIN request_infos ri ON t.request_info_id = ri.id LEFT JOIN users u ON t.user_id = u.id LEFT JOIN products p ON t.product_id = p.id ORDER BY t.created_at DESC LIMIT #{limit}
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tests WHERE created_at >= DATE_FORMAT(DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 MONTH), '%Y-%m-01') AND created_at < DATE_FORMAT(NOW(), '%Y-%m-01')
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tests WHERE created_at >= DATE_FORMAT(NOW(), '%Y-%m-01')
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tests WHERE stage = 'COMPLETED' AND DATE(updated_at) = DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 1 DAY)

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tests WHERE stage IN ('REQUEST', 'RECEIPT', 'RECEIPT_APPROVAL', 'RESULT_INPUT') AND DATE(created_at) <= DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 1 DAY) AND (updated_at IS NULL OR DATE(updated_at) <= DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 1 DAY))
